description_long: This object is a large, quartered section of a watermelon with a thick, dark green rind and a vibrant red, juicy interior. The rind displays a mottled pattern of lighter and darker green patches, with some tan blemishes and a small, pale spot near the base. The exposed interior reveals a moist, fibrous red flesh dotted with several brown and black seeds, some embedded and some slightly protruding. The cut surface is uneven and natural, with visible textural details and color variations typical of a freshly cut watermelon. The overall shape is a rounded wedge, with the rind forming a curved outer shell and the cut face forming a broad, circular surface. This object would commonly be found in urban environments such as markets, parks, or picnic areas, possibly as discarded food waste or part of a street vendor's offering.
description: A large, quartered wedge of watermelon with a mottled dark green rind and a juicy red interior dotted with seeds. The cut face is broad and circular, showing the fibrous flesh and seed distribution.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved, mottled green rind and a partial glimpse of the red, seeded interior.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the dark green, mottled rind with a small pale spot near the base and no visible interior.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the rind curving around and a clear edge of the red flesh and seeds visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view of the cut face, displaying the full red interior with scattered seeds and the pale rind border.
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern of irregular, intermingled dark and light green patches on the rind.
attribute_rind_markings: Tan, irregular blemishes and a small pale spot near the base; scattered and unevenly distributed.
attribute_seed_visibility: Seeds visible on the interior cut face, scattered, brown and black, about 8-10 in total.
